author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add GitHub Pages project site and fix missing Phase 6 in README

New docs/index.html tells the project story in 7 chapters: the problem,
why Temper, the 5 defense layers, literate programming, the 3-bug fix
story, the AI development comparison, and the 6-app proof. Styled in
black-and-white matching the pitch decks.

README.md adds missing Phase 6 (Changeset Enrichment) security analysis
section between Phase 5 and Phase 7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Refocus MITRE CWE analysis to SQL-only scope

Remove non-SQL CWEs (memory safety, XSS, CSRF, auth, etc.) from
cumulative mapping tables in RESEARCH.md and README.md. The ORM is
a SQL generation library — non-SQL concerns belong to the app layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add Phase 7 MITRE analysis and update docs with schema enrichment features

Updates README with Phase 7 security analysis table, SqlDefault type docs,
schema enrichment API section, and 184-test count. Adds Phase 7 section to
RESEARCH.md with component analysis and 6 CWE findings (all None severity).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add changeset enrichment (Phase 6) and MITRE security research document

Phase 6: 11 new Changeset methods (putChange, getChange, deleteChange,
validateInclusion, validateExclusion, validateNumber, validateAcceptance,
validateConfirmation, validateContains, validateStartsWith, validateEndsWith),
NumberValidationOpts class. 34 new tests, all 170 tests pass.

RESEARCH.md: Comprehensive MITRE CWE Top 25 analysis for all phases (1-6)
with per-component security assessment tables.

README.md: Updated with Phase 3-5 feature docs, security analysis sections
for aggregation, set ops, batch UPDATE/DELETE, and extended query features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add extended query features: NULLS FIRST/LAST, CROSS JOIN, row locking (Phase 5)

- NullsPosition sealed interface with NullsFirst/NullsLast for ORDER BY control
- CrossJoin type with nullable onCondition in JoinClause
- LockMode sealed interface with ForUpdate/ForShare for row-level locking
- Query constructor grows to 12 params (adds lockMode)
- 8 new tests, all 136 tests pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add batch UPDATE and DELETE query builders with safety guards

Phase 4 of Ecto feature clone. Adds UpdateQuery and DeleteQuery classes
with where/orWhere/limit/set/toSql methods. Both bubble (throw) if no
WHERE clause is present, preventing accidental full-table mutations.
Factory functions: update(), deleteFrom(). All 128 tests pass (13 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add set operations (UNION, INTERSECT, EXCEPT) and subqueries

Phase 3 of Ecto feature clone. Adds unionSql, unionAllSql, intersectSql,
exceptSql free functions, plus subquery, existsSql helpers and
whereInSubquery method on Query. All 115 tests pass (10 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add aggregation: GROUP BY, HAVING, DISTINCT, selectExpr, aggregate functions

Phase 2 of Ecto feature clone. Adds 6 aggregate free functions (countAll,
countCol, sumCol, avgCol, minCol, maxCol), selectExpr for expression-based
SELECT, GROUP BY, HAVING/orHaving, DISTINCT, and countSql convenience method.
Query constructor grows from 7 to 11 params. All 105 tests pass (20 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Robert Grayson
message:
Add WHERE clause enrichment: OR, NULL, IN, NOT, BETWEEN, LIKE/ILIKE

Phase 1 of Ecto feature clone. Adds WhereClause sealed interface
(AndCondition/OrCondition) for mixed AND/OR logic, plus 8 convenience
methods: orWhere, whereNull, whereNotNull, whereIn, whereNot,
whereBetween, whereLike, whereILike. All 85 tests pass (21 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
